title: Agarose–gelatin double embedding prior to paraffin processing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7fj5l","text":"This protocol details an agarose–gelatin double embedding workflow to immobilize small tissue fragments or spheroids for routine paraffin embedding. Samples are embedded in a 2% agarose–5% gelatin mixture, then fixed in neutral buffered formalin (NBF) and processed by standard FFPE methods. Compared with conventional methods, it facilitates handling of small specimens, preserves orientation, and minimizes sample damage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6s80l","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a05qk","text":"-Ensure that the agarose–gelatin solution is well mixed and not solidified before embedding.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5djqb","text":"-Prevent the sample from drying out.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ph9n","text":"-Process samples in small batches to avoid solidification of the agarose–gelatin gel.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52eig","text":"-If the agarose–gelatin gel does not fully cover the sample, add additional solution to embed completely.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4l4vv","text":"-Ensure all reagents and materials are ready before you begin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42b1h","text":"-Verify that the samples are properly prepared.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7urd5","text":"-Confirm the water bath and microwave oven are working properly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8drp9","text":"-Exercise caution to avoid burns when handling heated gels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ehoo2","text":"-During fixation, use appropriate equipment to prevent exposure to fixatives.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare a 4 Mass / % volume agarose solution( 200 µL per sample) : add agarose powder to distilled water (DW) in a 50 mL conical tube, heat in a microwave oven until fully dissolved.
Keep the 4 Mass / % volume agarose solution in the 60 °C water bath.

Prepare a 10 Mass / % volume gelatin solution: add gelatin powder to a volume of DW equal to 1.2x the volume of the 4 Mass / % volume agarose solution in a 50 mL tube. A larger volume of gelatin solution is required because some loss may occur during transfer of the viscous solution.
Place the 10 Mass / % volume gelatin solution in the 60 °C water bath for 00:20:00 to dissolve completely.
While maintaining 60 °C , mix the 10 Mass / % volume gelatin solution with the 4 Mass / % volume agarose solution at a 1:1 volume ratio using a serological pipette.
Adjust the water bath temperature to 40 °C to cool the final 2 Mass / % volume agarose– 5 Mass / % volume gelatin solution.
Transfer small tissue fragments or spheroids into Microtubes and wash twice with DPBS.

Add > 20× sample volume of neutral buffered formalin (NBF) solution to the tube and fix at Room temperature for 00:10:00 - 00:30:00 , depending on sample size. If the sample is not fixed before double embedding, it may be distorted by water imbibition.
Wash the sample twice with DW, each for 00:05:00 .
Place the sample onto a Petri dish. Transfer the samples with a small amount of water using a pipette with the tip cut off to minimize damage.
Remove excess water around the sample with tissue paper. Proceed immediately to the next step before the sample dries.
Add 200 µL of 2 Mass / % volume agarose– 5 Mass / % volume gelatin solution onto the sample. Keep the area around the sample free of air bubbles.
Using pipet tips or forceps, position the sample at the center and adjust it so that the desired cutting direction faces downward.
Allow the gel to solidify at Room temperature for 00:40:00 .

Trim the solidified gel into a pyramid shape (~25 mm³ base) using a blade. The pyramid shape helps maintain the orientation of the sample during subsequent processing. McClelland KS, Ng ET, Bowles J (2016). Agarose/gelatin immobilisation of tissues or embryo segments for orientated paraffin embedding and sectioning.. Differentiation; research in biological diversity. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.diff.2015.12.001
Transfer the pyramid-shaped gel into a Microtube containing 1 mL of NBF solution. Unfixed gels tend to stick together; therefore, fixation must be performed in separate Microtubes for each sample.
Proceed with routine paraffin embedding according to standard procedures.
creator: songh
